title: The King in Yellow
author: Chambers, Robert W. (Robert William)
summary: Set in an altered near-future America, a narrator recovers from a fall and describes national reforms, the sinister spread of a banned play, The King in Yellow, and the opening of a government Lethal Chamber. Haunted by the play’s second act, the narrator recalls reading it and seeing Carcosa and the pallid mask. At Washington Square the narrator witnesses the Chamber’s inauguration amid military spectacle. He later visits Hawberk’s armourer shop, meeting Constance and her father, and mentions friends and officials. The narrative blends civic satire, eerie literature-induced obsession, and hints of personal and social decay, and mounting uncanny dread.

characters.name: Mr. Castaigne
characters.personality: former idle young man turned ambitious, literary, prone to obsession
characters.lifespan: alive in narrative; survives fall and reads the play; haunted by its images

characters.name: Dr. Archer

characters.lifespan: alive; treated narrator and released him

characters.lifespan: alive; meets narrator in armourer shop

characters.name: Hawberk

characters.lifespan: alive; working in his shop repairing armour
